1.11 The Municipal Accountant concurred with the audit observation/s when discussed
during the exit conference and committed to complying with the audit
recommendations.
1.12 We will monitor the compliance of Management with the recommendations as
herein cited.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Make the necessary adjusting entries to restore the cash in bank balance and corresponding adjustments and
2. Fast track the full validation of the identified reconciling items for preparation of additional adjustments and journal entries to account the same in the books.
observations.reason:
Still not adjusted.
No valid justifications were provided by the MA aside from the lack of manpower since adjusting entries could be made immediately without the need of additional staff.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
3. Reclassify the non-current portion of Receivable Accounts for better presentation of receivables of the LGU in the Statement of Financial Position as at year-end reporting date;
4. Assess and validate the collectability of receivables and provide allowance for impairment based on the results of the assessment as required under IPSAS 29; and
5. Require a regular review, reconciliation and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the books. Analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and take necessary actions to drop them from the books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: No valid justifications were provided by the MA aside from the lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
6. Analyze and record the proposed adjusting/correcting entries in the books to rectify the noted overstatement/understatement of the RPT and SET Receivables and its related accounts; and
7. Follow the correct accounting entries regarding prompt/advance payments of real property and special education taxes in order to reflect the correct amount of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
Still not adjusted.
No valid justifications were provided by the MA aside from the lack of manpower since adjusting entries could be made immediately without the need of additional staff.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
8. Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the properties below the capitalization threshold and ensure that all future acquisitions of properties below the ₱15,000.00 capitalization threshold will be recorded in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006; and
9. Provide adjustments in the books of accounts (for improperly classified asset accounts) to properly classify the balances in the financial statements and in subsequent transactions, shall refer to the Revised Chart of Accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 for the description of account titles in order to properly record the financial transactions.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is waiting for the Inventory Committee to finish their physical count so that he can properly account for those PPE whose costs are below the threshold and drop them into the PPE account.

observations.recommendation: 15. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the actual amounts presented in the financial statements and disclose the said reconciliation on the face of the SCBAA or in the notes to financial statements in compliance with Item 47 of the IPSAS 24, to enable the Municipality to better discharge its accountability obligations.
observations.reason: There is no disclosure in the Notes to FS.
The Municipal Accountant recorded the payments of SRA to Hazard Pay account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection efforts of her office and should coordinate with other LGU officials to devise collection strategies and avail of all legal remedies provided under RA No. 7160 to collect all taxes due to the LGU.
observations.reason: The collection efficiency during the year remains low.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
27. Effect payment of goods and services through checks payable directly to the suppliers or dealers, and refrain from procuring goods and services through reimbursement basis, except if the disbursements are petty in nature and may be charged to Petty Cash Fund;
28. Comply with the requirement of adequate procurement planning in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 9184; and
29. Consider the setting-up of Petty Cash Fund duly supported by an estimate of expenses which shall be strictly for petty operating expenses in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason:
There were still reimbursements observed during the year.
The management did not set-up Petty Cash Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
30. Reconcile the discrepancies totaling ₱267,694.64 between the amount withheld and the amount remitted for the current year;
31. Reconcile the previous years’ outstanding balance of ₱56,428.11; and
32. Remit immediately the reconciled balance of Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts as at December 31, 2021.
observations.reason: Still not reconciled, reason cited is due to lack of manpower in the office of the MA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the LGU submit immediately the required authority from the SB for the expenditures totaling ₱1,473,644.84 charged to “Other COVID-19 related expenses” under the 20% DF.

We recommended that the LCE require:
41. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer to prepare in their individual monthly RAAF and without on time; and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to closely monitor the submission by the OIC-MT of the individual monthly RAAF and submit to OIC-MT their individual RAAF on time.
42. The Municipal Accountant-Designate to closely monitor the submission by the OIC-MT of the report of collection and deposits with the CRAAF to facilitate the required monthly reconciliation of accounting records with the treasury records.
observations.reason: Due to the delay in submission of collections by the collectors, the Treasurer cannot regularly prepare and submit the individual monthly RAAF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct all personnel concerned to immediately comply with the preparation of records/reports required of them, specifically stated in Items VI and VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008. Also, initiate close coordination for the immediate reconciliation of documents to ensure the accuracy of all records and reports.
observations.reason: Still not complied by the concerned personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
46. Reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts by tracing and analyzing transactions made in prior years’ and immediately prepare necessary adjustment of the reconciliations. Further, immediately prepare necessary adjustment of the adjustments; and
47. Closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in both funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission.
observations.reason: Still no reconciliations made. No valid justifications were provided by the MA aside from lack of manpower since reconciling items could be done without the need of additional staff.

observations.recommendation:
49. Prepare GAD Plan and Budget in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2016-01 dated June 12, 2016.
50. Update the GAD Database and provide gender related information with minimum GAD indicators that are matched to the biophysical, socioeconomic, and built environment components of the LGU Ecological Profile.
51. Require the GAD Focal Person to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in the inclusion of only DILG approved GAD PB in the Annual Budget and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in accomplishing the GAD Accomplishment Report with respect to the amounts to be presented in the said report.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Report were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Sangguniang Bayan should immediately revise the Municipal Revenue Code of 2000 as provided under Section 191 of RA No. 7160 to impose a more realistic rate that could give additional income and maximize revenue sources.
observations.reason: The previous audit recommendation has already been agreed that the Sangguniang Bayan pending approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the deadline on the monthly remittance of creditable withholding taxes on or before the 10th day of the following month the withholding was made.
observations.reason: There was still a delay on the remittance observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be required to strictly observe the timely submission of the same in accordance with Section 347 of RA No. 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD No. 1445. This recommendation is without prejudice to the result of post audit of the transactions.
observations.reason: There were still delays in the submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
55. Require the members of the MSWMB to review and update the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan with timeline for implementation to determine the PPA’s to be prioritized during the budget year.
56. Likewise, to intensify efforts to have the Solid Waste Management Plan approved by the NSWMC as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.
57. Further, enforce the remaining barangays to create a Barangay Solid Waste Management (BSWM) committee and prepare BSWMP as required.
58. Furthermore, require the personnel concerned to process the transfer of land title in the name of the municipality and enforce the supplier of the heavy equipment the issuance of the OR/CR for its immediate utilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MA-Designate strictly withhold the correct amount of taxes from all honoraria payments in accordance with Section 2.79 of RR No. 2-98 as amended by Section 3 of RR No. 10-2008.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still did not withhold taxes from the payment of honoraria.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the GAD Focal Point System require all concerned to implement the GAD PPAs and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report in the prescribed format under Annex E or E-1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-001. Further ensure that all PPAs with relevant gender issues are implemented and the GAD Budget and Accomplishment Reports are reconciled.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report were not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
65. We recommended that the BAC submit the lacking documents relative to the procurement of farm-to-market road projects to ascertain the propriety and legality of the payments for progress billings.
66. Likewise, require the BAC to judiciously undertake the evaluation of bids and post-qualification process in accordance with Sections 12, 23 and the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure that contracts are awarded only to bidders who passed the criteria for eligibility check post-qualification and thus be considered as the bidder with the lowest calculated and responsive bid.
observations.reason: There were still pending documents relative to the procurement.

observations.recommendation:
1.18 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Require all the collectors to remit all their collections daily and in case
of collectors assigned to the field, at least once a week or as soon as
the collections reach ₱5,000.00 to the LO and prepare the RCD at the
close of the business day or on the next working day;
2. Deposit intact all collections remitted to her by the LO;
3. Avoid issuing new sets of AFs until all collections were remitted in
full by the collectors;
4. Strengthen the internal control in cash management by observing the
proper cut-off in the reporting of collections, particularly the
preparation of RCD; and
5. Instruct all the collecting officers to ensure completeness and
accuracy of the information on the AFs and RCDs such as
signatures and dates.
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
above-mentioned errors.

2.20 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to immediately reconcile the cashbook balances
with the GL to ensure the accuracy of the cash accounts;
b. Municipal Accountant to:
1. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the unrecorded
receipts under the books of accounts of the TF;
2. Prepare and maintain SLs for all bank accounts and immediately
submit to the Audit Team the required subsidiary schedules of GL
balances of cash accounts for all funds and SLs per depository
bank; and
3. Prepare and timely submit the monthly BRS for all accounts
maintained by the Municipality and ensure that balances reflected
in the BRS are reconciled with the balances as shown in the GL.

4.14 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE direct the:
a. MHO to:
1. Record all donations-in-kind received by the RHU in the
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances,
duly supported with the (1) Acknowledgment Receipts, (2) proof of
receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries not necessarily in the
required form, and (3) the inventory of remaining undistributed
items, if any;
2. Submit a copy of the same to the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant.
b. Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Assist the MHO in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-
in-kind, and in the conduct of inventory of the remaining
undistributed items, if any; and
2. Maintain copies of the Summary/List of Donations Received,
Distributed and Balances and its supporting documents; and
3. Prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated Relief Goods for
DRRM.
c. Municipal Accountant – Designate to:
1. Based on the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM, make a
disclosure in the Notes to FS as to the quantity of received, issued
and balance of all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality,
with categorization as to food items, medicines and medical
supplies, hygiene kits, and others.

5.26 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE direct the:
a. Inventory Committee/Property Custodian to:
1. Formulate plans and strategize the complete conduct of physical
count of all its PPE, and prepared and submit the RPCPPE not
later than January 31 of the ensuing year in compliance with
Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
2. Reconcile their RPCPPE with the records of the Municipal
Accountant;
3. Ensure the report’s usability by including all the required
information in the RPCPPE, as illustrated in the MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume II; and
4. Prepare and submit the IIRUP and other required documents in
accordance with the Manual on PPE Appraisal and Disposal to
facilitate the disposal of all unserviceable properties at the earliest
possible time, pursuant to Section 79 of PD No. 1445, Sections 379-
381 of RA 7160, and in accordance with the procedures cited in
COA Circular No. 89-296.
b. Municipal Accountant to:
1. Verify and analyze the discrepancies, and prepare adjustments to the
affected records; and
2. Provide depreciation for depreciable PPE with an aggregate cost
of ₱54,619,840.40 in accordance with Paragraphs 43 and 71 of
IPSAS 17.
c. Create an Inventory Committee, who are temporarily relieved of all
their regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3)
months or less, and to prepare and submit the approved Physical
Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days
before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and ensure full
implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided
for in COA Circular 2020-006.

6.13 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and other concerned offices to coordinate with the
suppliers and employees to inform them of the unclaimed payables,
otherwise provide certification to the MAO that there are no valid
claimants for the long outstanding payables to facilitate the appropriate
reversion of funds.
b. Municipal Accountant to:
1. Regularly check, monitor and keep the Financial Liabilities and
Other Payables accounts updated and adequate;
2. Evaluate unclaimed obligations totaling ₱29,894.10 from the GF and
SEF that remained outstanding for two years or more. In the event
that no actual claim has been filed, whether administrative or judicial
or payables are not covered by perfected contracts or records,
recommend for the reversion of the same; and
3. Effect the necessary adjusting entries for the misclassified
accounts, if warranted, pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular No.
2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

7.5 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE require the
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Net Cash Flows from Operating
Activities to Surplus/Deficit and disclose the said reconciliation in the Notes
to the Financial Statements in compliance with Paragraph 29 of IPSAS 2.

8.24 We recommended, that the Management agreed, that the LCE:
a. Allocate funds for Disaster Recovery and Rehabilitation area as this is
also important in disaster and risk management;
b. Direct the MDRRMO to:
1. Judiciously prepare the AIP for LDRRMF incorporating time
schedules, availability of resources and other contingencies to avoid
delay in the implementation of PPAs;
2. Ensure and expedite the implementation of identified PPAs, to
strengthen the agency’s capacity in mitigating and preparing for,
responding to and recovering from the impact of disasters;
c. Direct the MPDC, MDRRMC and MDRRMO to provide SMART
performance indicators in each PPA appropriated for LDRRMF;
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer transfer
the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE for CY 2022
to the STF and ensure that, henceforth, Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular
No. 2012-002 pertaining to transfers of unexpended LDRRMF from the
GF to TF are strictly observed;
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Identify the cause or reason of the variance and make the necessary
adjusting entries, if any, to be able to present fairly the balance of the
Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF account in the Financial Statement;
2. Immediately submit the 41 lacking DVs and supporting documents
related to LDRRMF to the Office of the Auditor, to preclude
suspensions/disallowances in the audit; and
3. Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the
balances of unexpended LDRRMF as shown in Annex D of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 for fair presentation and to enhance the
understandability of the users of financial statements.

9.24 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE direct the:
a. Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal
Planning Coordinator to:
1. Plan the procurement and implementation of the identified
priority development projects under the 20% DF to ensure the
completion of the PPAs;
2. Coordinate with the beneficiaries/concerned offices with the PPAs
that will be included in the 20% DF AIP to determine specific
works to be done in each PPAs and to avoid delays in the
implementation; and
3. Evaluate the feasibility of the particular PPA and observe
timeliness in the implementation to achieve the desired socio-
economic development and environmental outcomes.
b. Municipal Engineer and the BAC to periodically provide the MBO and the
Municipal Accountant with the status of projects and the
procurements undertaken by the Municipality to assist the concerned
offices in the periodic review and monitoring of the balances of current
and continuing appropriations and inform immediately the MBO and
Municipal Accountant whenever there are changes on the awarded
PPAs and problems in the project’s implementation to make the
necessary adjustment in the financial reports relative to the affected
PPAs.

11.13 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE the Human
Resource Management Officer to:
a. Coordinate with the heads of the departments and offices to prepare a
contract incorporating a well-defined job description stating therein the
specific work to be assigned to each JO personnel; and
b. Submit a copy of Detailed Individual Accomplishment Reports to
facilitate comparison with the job description stated on their contracts,
thereby, ensuring the protection of government funds from unnecessary
expenditures.

12.7 We recommended, and the Mangement agreed, that the LCE direct the
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately release
the respective Province and Barangays’ share. Henceforth, ensure the on-
time remittance of the Province and Barangay’s share from RPT as provided
under Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
10. Fairly present the current and non-current portion of its outstanding Loans Payable; and
11. Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary loan details such as, the terms and condition of the loan which includes the interest rate, maturity date and quarterly/semi-annual amortization to enhance the understandability of the users of financial statements.
observations.reason: There is no disclosure in the Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
12. Ensure that Continuing Appropriations/Capital Outlay of DRRMF will continue to be recorded under the General Fund books until the projects funded therefor are completed;
13. Set-up and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the STF for year of unutilized DRRMF receipts by year of transfer, DRRMF from other LGUs by LGU, and DRRMF from other sources by donor in order to readily determine the balances of transfers/receipts; and
14. Reconcile and correct the Notes to Financial Statements by reflecting the correct balances of STF by year.
observations.reason: There is no disclosure in the Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
22. Issue an EO creating the M&E Team to assist the GFPS-TWG Chairperson and MSWD in establishing a gender-responsive M&E system; and
23. Incorporate in the CY 2023 GPB, the M&E Team’s activities to gather the much-needed data and information essential for establishing and enhancing the LGU GAD policies, programs, and projects.
observations.reason: The Management failed to incorporate the activities in the GPB.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
24. Strictly adhere to the new Policies and Guidelines on Overtime Services and Overtime Pay for Government Employees under CSC and DBM Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2015 dated November 25, 2015 and submit Report on Overtime Services with Pay to DBM’s regional office copy furnished the Civil Service Commission (CSC);
25. Formulate a detailed and systematic plan of activities per department. The planned activities shall integrate therewith the volume and frequency of regular and routine tasks to be performed to ensure that overtime services will be avoided; and
26. Make sure that infrequent overtime services have proper and prior authorization before the conduct of the same and stating thereupon the detailed overtime work program and ensure that overtime claims shall be supported by quantified accomplishment report.

33. Henceforth, record relief goods procured as Welfare Goods for Distribution and follow the perpetual recording inventories and maintain SLCs.
The Municipal Treasurer/Property and Supply Custodian-Designate to:

observations.recommendation:
36. Submit the Appropriation Ordinance with the Supplemental Budget and the supporting documents for the payment of Hazard Pay under Personnel Services amounting to ₱141,260.00 and additional COVID-19 hazard pay amounting to ₱1,591,260.00, and

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC Secretariat to post all the procurement transactions in CY 2020 totaling ₱30,923,940.54 undertaken through Emergency Procurement in the GPPB Online Portal as required under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.

43. Direct all personnel concerned to immediately comply with the preparation of records/reports required of them, specifically stated in Items VI and VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
44. Also, initiate close coordination for the immediate reconciliation of documents to ensure the accuracy of all records and reports.

48. Include in a separate caption in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, the PPAs charged to prior years’ unutilized LDRRM Fund in accordance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the GAD Focal Point System do a thorough assessment in identifying gender issues and corresponding activities that directly promote gender equality and women empowerment and to strictly adhere with the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

63. Limit the incurrence of expenditures for the use of mobile phones to what is authorized per Annual Budget Appropriation; and
64. Likewise, formulate guidelines for the entitlement of reimbursements for mobile expenses to avoid excessive expenditures on the use of mobile phone.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Make the necessary adjusting entries to restore the cash in bank balance
and recognize the corresponding liability for the stale checks; and
b. Fast track the full validation of the identified reconciling items for
preparation of necessary adjustments and journal entries to account the
same in the books.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Reclassify the non-current portion of Receivable Accounts for better
presentation of receivables of the LGU in the Statement of Financial
Position as at year-end reporting date;
b. Assess and validate the collectability of the receivables and provide
allowance for impairment based on the results of the assessment as
required under IPSAS 29; and
c. Require a regular review, reconciliation and monitoring of all booked
receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the
books. Analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and
take necessary actions to drop them from the books in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Analyze and record the proposed adjusting/correcting entries in the
books to rectify the noted overstatement/understatement of the RPT and
SET Receivables and its related accounts; and
b. Follow the correct accounting entries regarding prompt/advance
payments of real property and special education taxes in order to reflect
the correct amount of affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Trace the period or date of acquisition of the assets and compute the
depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided
under COA Circular 2003-007 and prepare the necessary adjusting
journal entries to update the carrying value of the said assets;
b. Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the properties below the
capitalization threshold and ensure that all future acquisitions of
properties below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 will be
recorded in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006; and
c. Provide adjustments in the books of accounts (for improperly classified
asset accounts) to properly classify the balances in the financial
statements and in subsequent transactions, shall refer to the Revised
Chart of Accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated
December 1, 2015 for the description of account titles in order to properly
record the financial transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Fairly present the current and non-current portion of its outstanding
Loans Payable; and
b. Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary loan details
such as, the terms and condition of the loan which includes the interest
rate, maturity date and quarterly/semi-annual amortization to enhance
the understandability of the users of financial statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Ensure that the unexpended balance of QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE at
the end of the year will be transferred to the STF Account and will be
recorded under the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account;
b. Ensure that Continuing Appropriations/Capital Outlay of DRRMF will
continue to be recorded under the General Fund books until the projects
funded therefor are completed;
c. Set-up and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the STF for unutilized
DRRMF by year of transfer, receipts of NDRRMF by transferring
agency, DRRMF from other LGUs by LGU, and other sources by donor
in order to readily determine the balances of transfers/receipts; and
d. Reconcile and correct the Notes to Financial Statements by reflecting the
correct balances of STF by year.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the actual
amounts presented in the financial statements and disclose the said
reconciliation on the face of the SCBAA or in the notes to financial statements
in compliance with Item 47 of the IPSAS 24, to enable the Municipality to
better discharge its accountability obligations.

We recommended and the LCE thru the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator agreed to properly evaluate, determine the
expected output and properly plan the schedule of implementation of the
unimplemented development PPAs, in line with its Local Development Plan.

We further recommended and the LCE agreed to regularly monitor their
implementation, in accordance with the approved AIP of the Municipal
Government.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
a. The concerned MHO personnel to submit immediately to the audit team,
thru the Accountant, the following documents to establish their claim to
SRAs under AO Nos. 36 and 42:
1. Authority for the entitlement of the allowance/ office order/
authority to report for work;
2. Daily Time Records with Biometric printouts/copies of logbook or
Certificate of Attendance in the COVID Facility/Center by their
immediate supervisor during the period of claims;
3. Accomplishment reports indicating the number of COVID patients
attended to in a day.
4. For health workers not directly catering COVID-19 patients, an
explanation as to the scientific basis for the premise that healthcare
workers not directly catering to COVID-19 patients are equally
exposed to COVID-19 and can be considered “in contact with
COVID-19 patients”, and thus should be given the SRA.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the payments of SRA to
permanent and contractual employees amounting to ₱478,636.28 from
Hazard Pay and Donations to Other Personnel Benefits and Other
Maintenance and Operating Expenses in the CY 2021 Statement of
Financial Performance.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to
intensify the collection efforts of her office and should coordinate with other
LGU officials to devise collection strategies and avail all legal remedies
provided under RA No. 7160 to collect all taxes due to the LGU.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. Issue an EO creating the M&E Team to assist the GFPS-TWG
Chairperson and MSWD in establishing a gender-responsive M&E
system; and
b. Incorporate in the CY 2023 GPB, the M&E Team’s activities to gather
the much-needed data and information essential for establishing and
enhancing the LGU GAD policies, programs, and projects.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. Strictly adhere to the new Policies and Guidelines on Overtime Services
and Overtime Pay for Government Employees under CSC and DBM Joint
Circular No. 1 series of 2015 dated November 25, 2015 and submit Report
on Overtime Services with Pay to DBM’s regional office copy furnished
the Civil Service Commission (CSC);
b. Formulate a detailed and systematic plan of activities per department. The
planned activities shall integrate therewith the volume and frequency of
regular and routine tasks to be performed to ensure that overtime services
will be avoided; and
c. Make sure that infrequent overtime services have proper and prior
authorization before the conduct of the same and stating thereupon the
detailed overtime work program and ensure that overtime claims shall be
supported by quantified accomplishment report.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Effect payment of goods and services through checks payable directly to
suppliers or dealers, and refrain from procuring goods and services
through reimbursement basis, except if the disbursements are petty in
nature and may be charged to Petty Cash Fund;
b. Comply with the requirement of adequate procurement planning in
accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 9184; and
c. Consider the setting-up of Petty Cash Fund duly supported by an estimate
of expenses which shall be strictly for petty operating expenses in
accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended and the LCE to require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Reconcile the discrepancies totaling ₱267,694.64 between the amount
withheld and the amount remitted for the current year;
b. Reconcile the previous years’ outstanding balance of ₱56,428.11; and
c. Remit immediately the reconciled balance of Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG
and PhilHealth accounts as at December 31, 2021.
